frontierComparison.interpretation: The 24-bucket post-successor q-avoiding source token is consumed with no survivors, but the emitted 24 post-post-successor boundary expands the open frontier. This is acceptable only as a ranked handoff; singleton q-child descent remains blocked until this assembly selects the whole-boundary next token.

assembledPieces.claim: All 718 source rows and all 5,058,399,114,142,580,922,880,572,195,875,967 post-successor q-avoiding source classes from the 24-bucket token have a later square-obstruction child by prime 283.
assembledPieces.boundary: This closes the source q-avoiding token as a no-survivor batch classification, but it emits post-post-successor root-child and post-post-successor q-avoiding rank obligations.

assembledPieces.claim: The post-post-successor square-obstruction children are partitioned into 24 buckets q in {151,157,163,167,173,179,181,191,193,197,199,211,223,227,229,233,239,241,251,257,263,269,271,283}, each with exactly two roots per source class.
assembledPieces.boundary: The 24 buckets are exact post-post-successor boundaries, not terminal closures.

assembledPieces.claim: The batch cover emits 10,116,798,228,285,161,845,761,144,391,751,934 post-post-successor root children.
assembledPieces.boundary: Those root children remain outside the assembled proof until a later packet compresses, closes, blocks, or ranks them.

assembledPieces.claim: The same 24 post-post-successor buckets emit 272,895,494,027,351,286,884,102,031,165,661,158,393 post-post-successor q-avoiding classes.
assembledPieces.boundary: Those classes require a compression theorem, structural decomposition, impossibility theorem, or deterministic ranked boundary before any singleton q-child descent.

supportedClaim: The post-successor 24-bucket q-avoiding cover is fully accounted as a no-survivor finite theorem-facing boundary, so the only allowed next selector-shaped work is whole-boundary compression, structural decomposition, impossibility theorem, or exact ranked boundary over all 24 post-post-successor buckets.

compressionCandidate.name: p443_q97_p479_later_prime_15_bucket_next_prime_17_bucket_post_next_20_bucket_successor_22_bucket_post_successor_24_bucket_post_post_successor_24_bucket_rank_compression
compressionCandidate.coveredFamily: All 24 post-post-successor obstruction-prime buckets emitted by the 24-bucket post-successor q-avoiding batch cover: 718 source rows, 5,058,399,114,142,580,922,880,572,195,875,967 source post-successor q-avoiding classes, 10,116,798,228,285,161,845,761,144,391,751,934 post-post-successor root children, and 272,895,494,027,351,286,884,102,031,165,661,158,393 post-post-successor q-avoiding classes.
compressionCandidate.whyThisCompresses: The q-avoiding cover proves row-uniform first post-post-successor obstruction and a shared two-root law for every stored source row. The remaining theorem-facing object is therefore the 24-prime post-post-successor rank surface, not any one q-child.
compressionCandidate.whyNotSingletonDescent: A q151, q157, or later singleton descent would consume only one emitted bucket while the frontier has expanded to 64 obligations. The finite token now available is the whole 24-bucket post-post-successor rank boundary.
compressionCandidate.failureBoundary: If no common compression theorem or impossibility theorem is visible, emit an exact deterministic 24-bucket ranked boundary packet with all buckets accounted, explicit survivors if any, and the next finite token.

remainingPuzzleBoundary.singletonDescentBlockedUntil: A whole-boundary compression, structural decomposition, impossibility theorem, or exact deterministic 24-bucket ranked boundary consumes the post-post-successor finite token.

oneNextAction.action: Compress the 24 post-post-successor obstruction-prime buckets into theorem-facing families, prove a structural rank decrease or impossibility theorem, or emit an exact deterministic 24-bucket ranked boundary before singleton q-child descent.
oneNextAction.failureBoundary: If the buckets do not compress structurally, write a deterministic ranked boundary packet over all 24 post-post-successor buckets and do not open q151/q157 singleton descent first.
oneNextAction.whyCheaperThanSingleSelector: The whole 24-bucket surface is already partitioned exactly and carries the same row-uniform two-root law; one whole-boundary rank/compression packet accounts for the finite token that every singleton child would otherwise fragment.
oneNextAction.completionRule: The next packet accounts for every one of the 24 post-post-successor buckets by compression theorem, structural decomposition, impossibility theorem, or exact deterministic ranked boundary.

nextTheoremMove: Compress the whole 24-bucket post-post-successor surface or emit an exact deterministic ranked boundary over all emitted buckets before singleton q-child descent.
proofBoundary: This packet is convergence assembly after the 24-bucket post-successor q-avoiding batch cover. It chooses a whole 24-bucket post-post-successor compression/ranked-boundary action and blocks singleton q-child descent. It does not close any emitted post-post-successor bucket, root child, q-avoiding class, unavailable complement, p4217 complement, collision-free matching obligation, or Problem 848.
